title: Jayalalithaa, Flying in the Sky, Does Not Know the Truth!
translatorNote: This translation is intended to carry Kalaignar's voice into clear, contemporary English rather than recast the letter as literary or academic prose. It preserves the source's argument, political directness, rhetorical questions, repetition, irony, factual detail, and paragraph order. Names, dates, figures, quotations, and intentional English expressions are retained. Where Tamil idiom cannot be reproduced literally without sounding unnatural, the English follows its sense and rhetorical force without adding claims absent from the source. The original Tamil is reproduced in full below the translation and remains the authoritative text. `Udanpirappē` is retained in Tamil transliteration rather than flattened into “brother,” “sister,” or “comrade.” Literally evoking “one born alongside me,” Kalaignar uses it as a distinctive address of shared identity, equality, affection, and solidarity within the movement. The source prints two deliberate boxed repetitions: Jayalalithaa's 1991 vow and 1992 Assembly statement, and the *Dinamani* editorial's account of the 1976 agreement. Both are preserved in their printed positions. The Tamil reading copy below repairs only unambiguous OCR defects confirmed against source pages 341-348; the canonical Tamil source remains unchanged.
paragraphs: Ever since Jayalalithaa began her election campaign, it appears she cannot feel satisfied with a speech unless she finds some fault to lay at my door! As far as we are concerned, however, her charges give us a welcome opportunity to explain the facts to the people of the country. Before they go to vote, let them read Jayalalithaa's accusations and also the detailed replies I give to them every day. Let them decide clearly what is true and on whose side justice lies!

Speaking at Aruppukkottai, the lady declared: “I emphatically charge that Karunanidhi was complicit in handing over Katchatheevu. As soon as an AIADMK Government is formed, Katchatheevu will be recovered through the Supreme Court and the rights of Tamil Nadu's fishermen will be secured.” So Katchatheevu is to be recovered once the AIADMK returns to office! This is the year 2016. Nearly a quarter of a century ago, yes, twenty-five years ago, on 15 August 1991, Chief Minister Jayalalithaa hoisted the flag at Fort St George for Independence Day and vowed, “I shall recover Katchatheevu at all costs.” What, then, has she been doing all these years? Why did she not recover Katchatheevu? Does she remember Katchatheevu only when an election arrives? And do you know what the very same Jayalalithaa, who said, “I shall recover Katchatheevu at all costs,” told the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly just eight months later, on 20 April 1992?

She said, “Recovering Katchatheevu does not appear to be something that can happen, or can happen soon.” That statement, delivered as though she were putting a full stop to its recovery, remains in the Assembly record even today.

One would have to count how many times this “lady” has issued statements claiming that I betrayed Tamil Nadu on this question! Every time, she begins piling up accusations that the Union Government and I joined together to give away Katchatheevu.

The DMK never consented, at any time, to giving away Katchatheevu; nor did it ever agree to such a transfer. I am ready to proclaim that challenge at any time and in any place! This is true not merely of the DMK as a party. While in government too, the DMK repeatedly raised the Katchatheevu question and never failed to demand, “Recover Katchatheevu for us.” The Kazhagam Government also never failed to point out how the fishermen's rights at Katchatheevu were being harmed and to demand a remedy. After I have explained all this repeatedly, what does it mean when Jayalalithaa raises the same accusations yet again? Are we not compelled to conclude that she has nothing else to speak about?
In a letter dated 30 September 1994 to the then Prime Minister, P.V. Narasimha Rao, Jayalalithaa, then Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu, wrote:
> “The ceding of this tiny island to the Island Nation had been done by the Government of India in the interest of better bilateral relations.”

In other words, she justified the transfer by saying that the Government of India had given this tiny island, Katchatheevu, to the island nation of Sri Lanka so that cordial relations could prevail between India and Sri Lanka. In my *Udanpirappu* letter of 19 July 2013, I asked whether Jayalalithaa had written this or not. Why has she not answered that question to this day? There is a proverb about “a thief being stung by a scorpion.” Is this another such case?

The agreement was signed on 28 June 1974. Is it true or not that, on the very next day, 29 June 1974, I convened a meeting of all-party leaders at the Tamil Nadu Secretariat in Chennai and passed a resolution on Katchatheevu? The AIADMK, however, refused even then to support and sign that resolution. In my letter to the Prime Minister on that same day, 29 June 1974, I wrote:

> Dear Prime Minister, > > On behalf of the Government of Tamil Nadu and on behalf of the people of Tamil Nadu, I am constrained to express our deep sense of disappointment over the recent Indo-Sri Lanka Agreement, according to which, Sri Lanka's claim to Katchathivu has been conceded by the Government of India. May I express the hope that you will take into consideration the decision contained in the Resolution and take appropriate action?
Is this the evidence that I gave Katchatheevu away? Is this the mark of my betrayal? Will Jayalalithaa offer an explanation?
In 1974, when the DMK was in office, the Kazhagam General Council passed a resolution declaring:

> “The Kazhagam General Council strongly condemns the agreement made by the Government of India granting sovereignty over Katchatheevu, which belongs to India and is closely connected with Tamil Nadu, to Sri Lanka. The DMK Government of Tamil Nadu approached the Union Government several times, with evidence, and tried to prevent this agreement from being made against the sentiments of the people of Tamil Nadu. Yet the Union Government disregarded those representations and gave Katchatheevu to Sri Lanka. This action causes us anguish.”

Nor did the DMK stop with passing a resolution. Is it false that, on 24 July 1974, the DMK held protests across Tamil Nadu over the Katchatheevu issue? Did I not personally take part, as Chief Minister, in the meetings held in Thanjavur district?

Nearly a quarter of a century ago, yes, twenty-five years ago, on 15 August 1991, Chief Minister Jayalalithaa hoisted the flag at Fort St George for Independence Day and vowed, “I shall recover Katchatheevu at all costs.” What, then, has she been doing all these years? Why did she not recover Katchatheevu? Does she remember Katchatheevu only when an election arrives? And do you know what the very same Jayalalithaa, who said, “I shall recover Katchatheevu at all costs,” told the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly just eight months later, on 20 April 1992? She said, “Recovering Katchatheevu does not appear to be something that can happen, or can happen soon.” That statement, delivered as though she were putting a full stop to its recovery, remains in the Assembly record even today.

When this issue came before Parliament on 23 July 1974, did our friend Era. Sezhiyan, then a DMK member, say or not say, “We shall not accept the surrender to Sri Lanka of Katchatheevu, which belongs to Tamil Nadu”? In the Rajya Sabha, did Kazhagam member and friend S.S. Marisamy declare or not declare, “It is an anti-democratic course for the Union Government to enter into this agreement unilaterally, without consulting the Tamil Nadu Government”? Can Jayalalithaa not understand any of these events?
At the very next sitting of the Legislative Assembly, on 21 August 1974, I moved the following resolution on behalf of the Government:

> “This House expresses its profound regret over the decision taken by the Union Government on the question of Katchatheevu, which belongs to India and over which Tamil Nadu has close rights. It urges the Union Government to reconsider the matter, to take steps to revise the agreement made with the Government of Sri Lanka so that India retains sovereignty over Katchatheevu, and to respect the sentiments of the people of Tamil Nadu.”
Are all these documents evidence of betrayal? Can Jayalalithaa not understand even this?

Jayalalithaa repeatedly asks why Karunanidhi did not lift even a blade of grass to recover Katchatheevu while the DMK was in office. But Jayalalithaa herself was in office from 1991 to 1996, from 2001 to 2006, and now for five years from 2011. I had asked why she did not recover Katchatheevu. Could she not have answered that question at least now, in the course of her long speech? Why was there no answer?
In an editorial published on 17 August 1991, the *Dinamani* newspaper wrote:

> “Under the agreement concluded in 1974 by the then Prime Minister Indira Gandhi with Mrs Bandaranaike, it was announced that Katchatheevu would be ceded to Sri Lanka in order to foster cordial relations between the two countries. Before the agreement was signed, the Union Government did not consult the people of Tamil Nadu about it.”
The same editorial said:

> “In 1976, during the Emergency in India, another agreement was made with the Sri Lankan Government without consulting the people. Through it, the traditional right of Indian fishermen to fish near Katchatheevu was also surrendered.”

This alone makes clear that the claim that fishing rights were surrendered while the DMK was in office is a complete lie and a fraud. If Jayalalithaa refuses to believe even an editorial in *Dinamani*, a newspaper so close to her, who can possibly cure her?
The editorial published by *Dinamani* on 17 August 1991 said:

> “In 1976, during the Emergency in India, another agreement was made with the Sri Lankan Government without consulting the people. Through it, the traditional right of Indian fishermen to fish near Katchatheevu was also surrendered.”

This alone makes clear that the claim that fishing rights were surrendered while the DMK was in office is a complete lie and a fraud. If Jayalalithaa refuses to believe even an editorial in *Dinamani*, a newspaper so close to her, who can possibly cure her?

Even after the DMK returned to office in 2006, I reminded Prime Minister Dr Manmohan Singh of the resolution that the AIADMK Government had brought before the Assembly in 1991. In my letter of 22 September 2006, I wrote:

> “I am sure you are also aware of the fact that the Legislative Assembly of Tamil Nadu had passed a Resolution in 1991 on this issue wherein the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly called upon the Government of India to take all urgent action and efforts necessary to get back Katchathivu and restore it to the Indian territory apart from moving the Sri Lankan Government to pay compensation for families of fishermen killed by the Sri Lankan Navy as well as those who have lost their belongings in the attacks by them. Government of India was also requested to move the Sri Lankan Government to create ‘Congenial conditions for Tamil Nadu fishermen to lead a peaceful life.’”
Is it right for Jayalalithaa to conceal all these reminders whenever it suits her?

Christians and others travel to Katchatheevu every year to take part in St Antony's festival. The festival had not been held after 1983. It was in 2010, while the Kazhagam was in office and I was Chief Minister, that I raised the matter with the Union Government and the church festival was held again.
In a letter dated 23 July 2003 to the then Prime Minister, Atal Bihari Vajpayee, Chief Minister Jayalalithaa wrote:

> “The best possible solution is to get the island of Katchathivu and adjacent seas on lease in perpetuity solely for fishing, drying of nets and pilgrimage. Sri Lanka's Sovereignty over Katchathivu could be upheld at the same time.”

That is, she proposed that Katchatheevu and its adjoining seas be obtained on a perpetual lease so that Tamil Nadu's fishermen could fish, dry their nets and undertake pilgrimages, while Sri Lanka's sovereignty over Katchatheevu could simultaneously be accepted. The very person who wrote then of a “lease” and “sovereignty” now stands at the Aruppukkottai meeting and fires a volley of questions at me!

Jayalalithaa herself said something in a statement she issued earlier: “The Katchatheevu question is a matter involving another country. The authority to recover it rests with the Union Government. Had a State Chief Minister possessed that authority, Katchatheevu would have been recovered that very day.” Why, then, does the Jayalalithaa who said this question me? Should she not question the Union Government? But then, she is someone who does not even make time to meet Union ministers!

Thus Jayalalithaa has already said that “only the Union Government has the authority to recover Katchatheevu”; that “Katchatheevu was given to Sri Lanka in order to foster cordial relations between India and Sri Lanka”; and that “Sri Lanka's sovereignty over Katchatheevu must be accepted.” Now, concealing all of this behind a curtain, she claims during an election that I gave Katchatheevu away and that she will recover it if returned to office. That is the real deceitful drama. The people may be fooled once or twice, but the people of this country are no longer prepared to believe Jayalalithaa's claim that she can keep opening bundles of lies and win through them forever. Jayalalithaa, still flying high in the sky, does not know that truth. She will know it only when she comes down to earth!
With affection,
M.K.
18 April 2016
